seoTitle: How to Export Records to PDF Using Airtable Gallery View
metaDescription: Export Airtable Gallery View cards as polished PDFs by pairing a card-based layout with the Page Designer extension for image-heavy documents.
intro: When your Airtable records are visual — product cards, portfolios, or catalogs — exporting from Gallery View through Page Designer keeps the card-based look intact in the final PDF instead of flattening everything into a plain table. This guide covers setting up the gallery layout and mapping it into a printable template.

steps.heading: Set up a Gallery View
steps.body: Create a clean, card-based Gallery View for your target table, ordering and hiding fields to optimize how each card looks.

steps.heading: Install Page Designer
steps.body: Go to the Extensions panel and install the native Page Designer module.

steps.heading: Match the layout to your card ratios
steps.body: Customize your document layout dimensions to mirror your Gallery View card proportions for a consistent look.

steps.heading: Map images and text to the template
steps.body: Drag and drop your image attachments and core text variables into the template to recreate the card design in document form.

steps.heading: Preview record alignment
steps.body: Toggle through record previews to check visual alignment and margin accuracy before exporting.

steps.heading: Print or save as PDF
steps.body: Use the extension's native print option to initiate creation, then configure system print settings to save individual cards or bulk sets as PDFs.

faq.question: Why use Gallery View instead of Grid View for PDF export?
faq.answer: Gallery View is card-based and better suited to visual, image-heavy records, so exporting from it preserves that layout instead of forcing data into rows and columns.

faq.question: Can I export just one card or does it have to be the whole set?
faq.answer: You can export individual cards or bulk sets, depending on how you configure the print settings in the final step.

faq.question: Do I need Page Designer installed to export from Gallery View?
faq.answer: Yes, Page Designer is the extension that handles the actual PDF template creation and export process.

faq.question: What kind of records work best with this method?
faq.answer: Records with images and a handful of key fields — like products, portfolio pieces, or profile cards — tend to translate best into a card-style PDF.
